personas.name: Multilingual Mental Health Seeker
personas.description: A non-english speaker that wants to use the app in thier native language and share insights with a therapist who also does not speak English.
goals: Access mental health tracking features in their native language
Share app insights with a therapist who does not speak English
pain_points: App content and UI are only available in English
No option to change the display language within the app
context: Using the app alongside therapy sessions
People with limited English proficiency trying to navigate an English-only interface
constraints: All UI elements and content must be available in at least one non-English language
Language selection must persist across sessions
personas.notes: Renamed from 'Mental Health Tracker' which was too generic and not grounded in the language group. The auto persona for G1 did not mention language at all despite being derived from a language support group.

personas.name: Budget-Conscious Wellness User
personas.description: A user looking for affordable mental health support without the burden of a paid subscription.
goals: Access core mental health features without paying
Understand what is free versus paid before investing time in the app
pain_points: Useful features and historical mood data are locked behind a paywall
No clear indication of pricing until after account setup
context: Downloaded the app seeking affordable mental health support
Willing to use the app long-term if the free tier provides enough value
constraints: Core mood tracking and insights must be accessible without a subscription
Pricing information must be visible before account creation
personas.notes: Kept core structure from auto persona, added missing context around pricing transparency and removed vague constraint about 'willing to use free alternatives' which is not a system constraint.

personas.name: Daily Self-Reflection User
personas.description: An engaged user who relies on the app daily for mood tracking and self-reflection as part of managing their mental health.
goals: Track mood and emotions consistently every day
Gain meaningful insights into mental health patterns over time
pain_points: App does not always surface actionable insights from tracked data
Some users find check-in questions feel repetitive over time
context: Using the app as a personal mental health management tool
May be using the app alongside professional therapy
constraints: The app must provide valuable insights derived from logged mood data
Check-in experience must feel engaging and do not repeat the questions on consecutive days
personas.notes: Renamed from 'Hopeful Helper' which was vague. Removed unsupported pain point about PTSD which was not grounded in the review group evidence. Refined goals and constraints to be more specific and requirements-oriented.

personas.name: Frequent Check-In User frustraed
personas.description: A user with high frequancy of check-in, bothered by repetitive questions in excesrsises.
goals: Meaningful daily check-ins that feel varied and relevant
Export and share mental health results with healthcare providers
pain_points: Check-in questions repeat too frequently, reducing engagement
Emotion options are too limited to accurately capture nuanced feelings
context: Uses the app regularly for structured mental health check-ins
Shares app results with a GP or psychiatrist
constraints: Check-in questions must not repeat on consecutive days
Emotion selection must include enough options to capture a range of feelings
personas.notes: Renamed from the ambiguous 'Hopeful Helper'. eliminated the PTSD pain point that was unsubstantiated by the review group's findings. improved objectives and limitations to be more precise and requirements-focused.

personas.name: Privacy-Conscious Long-Term User
personas.description: A long-term user who stores sensitive personal data in the app and is concerned about third-party data sharing and lack of security controls.
goals: Use the app knowing their sensitive journal and mood data is not shared with third parties
Have security controls such as a PIN to protect app access
pain_points: App shares device identifiers with third parties without an opt-out option
No PIN or biometric lock to prevent unauthorized access to personal data
context: Has been using the app long-term and stores sensitive mental health information
Reads privacy policies carefully and is aware of data practices
constraints: User data must not be shared with third parties without explicit opt-in consent
App must provide an access control mechanism such as a PIN or biometric lock
personas.notes: changed from Disappointed User, which was too ambiguous and more concerned with update annoyance than privacy. The entire persona was rewritten to fit the hybrid group H5's predominant focus of data security and privacy. Unsupported pain points about data loss upon updates were eliminated.
